title: Legacy (2006) 23
content:
{{Top|fa|rwm|lgc|title=''Legacy'' (2006) 23}} {{Youmayd|the [[Star Wars: Legacy|''Legacy'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]]|''Loyalties''|[[loyalties (disambiguation)]]}} {{ComicBook |image=[[File:SWLEG23_cover.jpg]] |title=''Legacy'' 23 |writer=[[John Ostrander]]''Legacy'' 23 |penciller=[[Jan Duursema]] |inker=[[Dan Parsons]] |letterer=[[Michael Heisler]] |colorist=[[Brad Anderson]] |cover artist=*Jan Duursema *Brad Anderson |editor=[[Randy Stradley]] |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |release date=*[[May 7]], [[2008]]{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/14-884/Star-Wars-Legacy-23|text=Star Wars: Legacy #23}} *[[December 28]], [[2011]] {{C|Digital release}}{{DarkHorse|subdomain=digital|url=profile/771.star-wars-legacy-23|text=Star Wars: Legacy #23 @ Dark Horse Digital}} |pages=40 |upc= |timeline=[[137 ABY]] |series=''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' |issue=23 }} {{Quote|Taking control of your future sometimes means cutting loose your past!|Publisher's summary|}} '''''Legacy'' 23''' is the twenty-third issue of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]''. It contains the story "'''Loyalties, Part 1'''." Within its pages, the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Cade Skywalker]] and a group of his allies storm the grounded [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Crimson Axe]]'' on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]] and retrieve another starship, the ''[[Grinning Liar]]'', from the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Rav]]. Rav afterward solidifies an alliance with the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lady]] [[Darth Maladi]], and deposed [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] prepares to send several of his [[Imperial Knight]]s on a mission to pursue Skywalker. His daughter [[Marasiah Fel|Marasiah]] and fellow Imperial Knight [[Antares Draco]] discuss their feelings for one another, and the crew of the ''Mynock'' arrive at the planet [[Iego/Legends|Iego]] to find the [[Rawk's Nest|home]] of their friend "Bantha" [[Rawk]] under [[Skirmish on Iego|attack]] from the criminals of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]]. They are pursued to Iego by a masked stranger named [[Azlyn Rae]]. The issue was written by [[John Ostrander]] and penciled by [[Jan Duursema]]. Its events conclude an existing storyline while simultaneously beginning to move pieces into position in advance of ''Legacy''{{'s}} contribution to the [[Dark Horse Comics]] [[Wikipedia:Fictional crossover|crossover event]], ''[[Star Wars: Vector]]''. 30,346 copies of ''Legacy'' 23 were [[Wikipedia:Direct market|released]] to comic shops worldwide on [[May 7]], [[2008]], which made it Dark Horse's third-highest selling comic of the month. The issue received generally positive feedback across the [[Wikipedia:Internet|internet]] following its release, and the following month's [[Legacy (2006) 24|''Legacy'' (2006) 24]] wrapped up ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Star Wars: Legacy: Loyalties|''Loyalties'']] story arc. In [[2009]], the comic was included in ''Legacy''{{'s}} fifth trade paperback, [[Star Wars: Legacy Volume 5: The Hidden Temple|''The Hidden Temple'']], and it was later part of the hardcover release ''[[Star Wars: Legacy Book 2]]'' in [[2013]]. The issue was released digitally on [[December 28]], [[2011]]. ==Plot summary== {{Quote|The pirate Rav has nearly every bounty hunter in the galaxy looking for Cade Skywalker, but he never anticipated what would happen if Cade came looking for him. Scores will be settled the unfriendly way.|Publisher's summary|}} In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[137 ABY]], [[Naxy Screeger]]—a debt-ridden [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] in the service of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Rav]][[Legacy (2006) 3|''Legacy'' (2006) 3]]—sneaks into the back door of Rav's [[Golden Gorg Cantina]], located within the grounded [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Crimson Axe]]'' on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]]. He is spotted by Rav and chastised for using the unmarked entrance, but Screeger reveals that he has encountered some friends: the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Cade Skywalker]] and [[Jariah Syn]], and the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Chak (Wookiee)|Chak]] and [[Kee (Devaronian)|Kee]], who come in with guns blazing in an effort to retrieve Chak and Kee's starship, the ''[[Grinning Liar]]''. Rav has been in possession of the ship since he betrayed Syn to [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]], and he responds by ordering a group of his cronies to attack the new arrivals. Skywalker and his allies make short work of their foes with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], and [[razor bug]]s, and Skywalker turns [[Kol Skywalker's lightsaber|his lightsaber]] on Rav. Rav offers him [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]], but Skywalker uses the threat of [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] to coerce Rav into swearing the loyalty [[Bloody Bones oath|oath]] of the [[Bloody Bones]] to him in retaliation for Rav's betrayal of Syn, a former member of his crew. Rav releases the ''Grinning Liar'' back into Chak and Kee's possession, and Skywalker and his allies depart the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] as a masked stranger watches them from the back door. [[File:Yarrrrrr.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Cade Skywalker shocks Rav into submission.]] Rav immediately contacts [[Darth Maladi]] of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith]] and reports Skywalker's whereabouts to her. She threatens [[Death/Legends|death]] if he is ever unfaithful to her, and he swears the oath of the Bloody Bones for the second [[Time/Legends|time]] in several [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. Meanwhile, on the [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], deposed [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] of the [[Empire-in-exile]] laments having let Skywalker, at one time his guest, leave the planet. Fel is worried that the bounty hunter betrayed Imperial secrets to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt during his recent stint as a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]], and he sends three [[Imperial Knight]]s—[[Ganner Krieg]], [[Antares Draco]], and his daughter [[Marasiah Fel|Marasiah]]—on a mission. Draco fails to answer his summons, choosing to instead sulk in the [[Imperial Headquarters|palace's]] greenhouses over his forbidden love for Marasiah. She seeks him out, however, and assures him that his feelings are reciprocated, kissing him to prove the point. As they prepare to depart Socorro in their respective ships, Skywalker exchanges farewells with Chak and Kee, who advise him to lose himself in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] underworld. Screeger is desperate for a ride offworld to spare him from Rav's wrath, and Skywalker tosses him into the smelly cargo hold of his ship, the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']]. He and Syn then prep the ship for a journey to [[Iego/Legends|Iego]] to visit their old friend "Bantha" [[Rawk]]. En route, Skywalker seeks out [[Deliah Blue]], the ship's [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] and a romantic interest of his, who is tinkering with the ship and is resentful of him for having become passionate with the [[Hand (One Sith)|Sith Hand]] [[Darth Talon]]. She eventually forgives him, and Skywalker shoos his [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], away as he and Blue share a tender moment. The ''Mynock'' arrives at Iego, although it is followed by the masked stranger from the cantina. Its crew finds [[Rawk's Nest|Rawk's home]] [[Skirmish on Iego|under attack]] by forces of the criminal organization [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], and Skywalker mans his ship's guns to help Rawk's family fight the attackers off. When the threat is neutralized, Rawk invites them in for dinner. The stranger makes planetfall and reports via [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] to an unseen ally on Skywalker's whereabouts. As she concludes her transmission, she mentions her name: [[Azlyn Rae]]. ==Development== {{Quote|We knew we wanted to have a big thing coming out of 'Vector.' And we figured taking Krayt out of the mix would make it a big event. It would be something that we could focus all of 2008 working up to. We spent all of 2008 moving the pieces into motion.|John Ostrander|}} In 2006, [[Dark Horse Comics]]' Vice-President of Publishing [[Randy Stradley]] conceived of a [[Wikipedia:Fictional crossover|crossover event]] occurring throughout all four ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series being published by Dark Horse at the time: [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']], [[Star Wars: Dark Times|''Dark Times'']], [[Star Wars: Rebellion (comic series)|''Rebellion'']], and [[Star Wars: Legacy|''Legacy'']]. His hope was that events in the crossover would genuinely affect the characters of each series, keeping existing fans satisfied while simultaneously attracting new ones.{{WebCite|url=http://forum.newsarama.com/showthread.php?t=133122|text=CROSSING IT OVER: STAR WARS VECTOR I: RANDY STRADLEY|work=Newsarama|archivedate=20090129230323}} [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]], the creative team behind ''Legacy'', helped Stradley plan the crossover, which was eventually dubbed ''[[Star Wars: Vector]]''. They were committed to telling a good story within ''Legacy''{{'s}} chapters of the tale.{{WebCite|url=http://forum.newsarama.com/showthread.php?t=133255|text=CROSSING IT OVER: MILLER, OSTRANDER & DUURSEMA ON STAR WARS: VECTOR|work=Newsarama|archivedate=20090129225912}} As a twelve-issue event that would be told in the pages of all four series throughout [[2008]], ''Vector'' would conclude within issues [[Legacy (2006) 28|28]]–[[Legacy (2006) 31|31]] of ''Legacy''. Ostrander and Duursema decided that the death of Darth Krayt, the series' primary antagonist, would be the large event that would bring ''Vector'' to a close. Accordingly, nearly every issue of ''Legacy'' published in 2008 served to build up to Krayt's death, moving various pieces of the story into motion.{{WebCite|url=http://www.comicbookresources.com/?page=article&id=19568|text=STAR WARS VECTOR PRIMED, PART II|work=Comic Book Resources|archivedate=20130918155516}} ''Legacy'' 23 introduces the character of Azlyn Rae, a character who would go on to play a large role in ''Legacy''{{'s}} chapters of ''Vector'' and deliver one of the killing strokes to Krayt.[[Legacy (2006) 28|''Legacy'' (2006) 28]][[Legacy (2006) 29|''Legacy'' (2006) 29]][[Legacy (2006) 30|''Legacy'' (2006) 30]][[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]] The comic is the twenty-third issue of ''Legacy'' and the first of two issues in the series' ''[[Star Wars: Legacy: Loyalties]]'' story arc. However, the "[[Blaster (department)|Blaster]]" department of the [[Star Wars Insider 101|101st issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'', released on [[May 6]], 2008, asserts that the arc covers Issues 22–23 and is titled ''Home''.{{InsiderCite|101|Blaster}} It is the first issue of ''Legacy'' since its [[Legacy (2006) 19|nineteenth]] to feature the bounty hunter Cade Skywalker,[[Legacy (2006) 19|''Legacy'' (2006) 19]] the series' principal protagonist.{{WebCite|url=http://www.newsarama.com/comics/star-wars-legacy-100615.html|text=Future's End: Creators Talk STAR WARS LEGACY Finale|work=Newsarama|archivedate=20131019102819}} The nineteenth issue saw Skywalker escape from the clutches of Darth Krayt, and ''Legacy'' 23 focuses on his actions and decisions immediately thereafter. Skywalker speaks several words of [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]] in the issue, and a short glossary appears within the comic's letters column. Ostrander consulted the [[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']],{{DHBoards|subdomain=www|url=Help/Boards/viewtopic.php?t=13508&postdays=0&postorder=asc&start=30|thread=LEGACY #23 -- Past tense, future not really any better|user=johnostrander|date=2008-05-11|time=7:28 pm}} published in [[2001]],[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']] in order to accurately portray the [[Language/Legends|language]]. The issue was written by Ostrander, penciled by Duursema, and edited by Stradley. [[Dan Parsons]] inked, [[Brad Anderson]] colored, and [[Michael Heisler]] provided lettering. Duursema and Anderson also designed the cover, which features a photograph of Skywalker, Jariah Syn, and Rav. While penciling it, Duursema attempted to invoke the feeling of a photograph from the [[Wikipedia:American Old West|American Old West]], hoping that it would reflect a happy time from Skywalker's past.{{JCFCite|url=posts/27957487|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #23: Loyalties, part 1 (of 2)|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2008-01-10|time=1:19 pm|archivedate=20200426081504}} [[Sean Cooke]] was also credited as Designer, [[Mike Richardson]] as Publisher, and [[Freddye Lins]] and [[Dave Marshall]] as Assistant Editors. First announced in [[January]] 2008, ''Legacy'' 23 was originally scheduled for a release date of [[April 16]],{{JCFCite|url=posts/27953165|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #23: Loyalties, part 1 (of 2)|archivedate=20200426081518}} but its publication was delayed by nearly one month due to compounded delays from previous ''Legacy'' issues.{{JCFCite|url=posts/27458571|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #19: Claws of the Dragon, part 6 (of 6)|archivedate=20200426081729}}{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/14-778/Star-Wars-Legacy-19-Claws-of-the-Dragon-part-6-of-6|text=Star Wars: Legacy #19-Claws of the Dragon part 6 (of 6)}} {{C|Issue 19's ultimate release date}} After a three-page teaser preview was published online by Dark Horse,{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/Previews/14-884|text=Star Wars: Legacy #23 Preview}} the issue was eventually [[Wikipedia:Direct market|released]] to comic shops on [[May 7]], 2008. Including advertisements, the issue is forty pages long; the story itself is twenty-two pages. The comic was later included in the trade paperback ''[[Star Wars: Legacy Volume 5: The Hidden Temple]]'', released on [[January 21]], [[2009]],{{DarkHorse|url=Books/15-834/Star-Wars-Legacy-Volume-5--The-Hidden-Temple-TPB|text=Star Wars: Legacy Volume 5—-The Hidden Temple TPB}} and the hardcover omnibus ''[[Star Wars: Legacy Book 2]]'', released on [[September 18]], [[2013]].{{DarkHorse|url=Books/23-429/Star-Wars-Legacy-Book-2-HC|text=Star Wars: Legacy Book 2 HC}} On [[December 28]], [[2011]], the issue was released digitally. ==Release and reception== 30,346 copies of ''Legacy'' 23 were shipped to comic shops worldwide for its May 2008 release. It was the seventy-second highest-selling comic issue of the month, and the third highest for Dark Horse.{{WebCite|url=http://www.comichron.com/monthlycomicssales/2008/2008-05.html|text=Comic Book Sales Figures for May 2008|author=[[John Jackson Miller]]|work=[https://www.comichron.com The Comics Chronicles]|archivedate=20080928222645}} ==Continuity== {{Quote|… when Cade confronted Krayt, Talon intervened. Cade ran her through with his lightsaber, killing her almost instantly.|''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'', contradicting ''Legacy'' 23 by claiming that Cade Skywalker killed Darth Talon|''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''}} In addition to setting up the events of ''Vector'', ''Legacy'' 23 also brings a storyline to a close. Chak and Kee had first appeared in ''Legacy'' in its [[Legacy (2006) 14|fourteenth issue]], where they revealed that Syn and Blue had borrowed the ''Grinning Liar'' before going missing.[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] [[Legacy (2006) 16|Issue 16]] established that Rav had betrayed Syn and Blue to Darth Krayt's Imperials and taken the ''Grinning Liar'' for himself.[[Legacy (2006) 16|''Legacy'' (2006) 16]] ''Loyalties, Part 1'' sees Chak and Kee retrieve their ship from Rav, and marks their final appearance within ''Legacy''{{'s}} pages. ''Legacy''{{'s}} nineteenth issue had seen Skywalker [[Shiak|impale]] Darth Talon with his lightsaber. Although ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', later released in [[December]] 2008, claims that the act killed her,{{CSWECite|III|215|Talon, Darth}} ''Legacy'' 23 depicts her floating in a [[Bacta tank/Legends|bacta tank]] behind Darth Maladi. A fully-recovered Talon had previously appeared in [[Legacy (2006) 22|''Legacy'' (2006) 22]], however.[[Legacy (2006) 22|''Legacy'' (2006) 22]] The planet Iego's appearance in the comic marks its first appearance in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity after first being mentioned in the [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].{{Film|I}} ==Legacy== {{Quote|Well, you gave me up for dead, didn't you, Cade?''"
"''Long time ago… ''Azlyn Rae''.|Azlyn Rae and Cade Skywalker, in the following issue of ''Legacy''|[[Legacy (2006) 24|''Legacy'' (2006) 24]]}} [[File:Azlynraeunmasked.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Azlyn Rae is unmasked in ''Loyalties, Part 2''.]] The various plotlines introduced in ''Legacy'' 23 continued in subsequent issues of ''Legacy'', affecting its story throughout 2008 and beyond. "Bantha" Rawk is revealed to be Skywalker's Uncle Nat in the subsequent [[Legacy (2006) 24|''Legacy'' (2006) 24]], and Azlyn Rae an Imperial Knight and childhood friend of Skywalker's. The mission undertaken by Draco, Krieg, and Marasiah sees them follow Skywalker to the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order's]] [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]][[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]] and join him on a [[Mission to Had Abbadon|mission]] to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Darth Krayt.[[Legacy (2006) 26|''Legacy'' (2006) 26]] Rae plays a large role in ''Legacy''{{'s}} remaining 2008 issues, also joining the assassination mission and rekindling an old romance with Skywalker during its course. She stabs Darth Krayt during ''Vector''{{'s}} [[Battle on Had Abbadon|climax]], directly contributing to his apparent death. Rav's partnership with Maladi sees him lure Skywalker into her clutches on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]] during a 2009–[[2010]] [[Star Wars: Legacy: Monster|story arc]],''[[Star Wars: Legacy: Monster]]'' and the ''Grinning Liar'' helps [[Evacuation of Dac|evacuate]] residents of the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] after the world is [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] by the Sith.[[Legacy (2006) 47|''Legacy'' (2006) 47]] ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Legacy (2006) 23}} ===Cover gallery=== File:SWLEG23_cover.jpg|Cover File:SWLegacy23-FC.png|Full cover art ===Collected in===
